reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Conor McGregor x Max Holloway não é apenas um duelo entre dois strikers de elite. A grande questão é: quanto uma paus
good: Compelling narrative hook about McGregor's 5-year layoff creates curiosity — driving 8.4s average watch time
Clear text overlay (McGregor × Holloway) immediately signals topic and stops scroll
Direct eye contact and varied facial expressions maintain momentum throughout
Strong share rate (238 shares, 3.7% of views) indicates content resonates with fight fans
bad: Critically low saves (2 total) despite 12.4% engagement — viewers don't save for reference despite high watch time
Static close-up commentary format throughout — missing actual McGregor/Holloway fight footage to support analysis
Opening hook shows reading reaction rather than showcasing the fighters being discussed
Unintentional lens flare/overexposed lighting in final frame appears amateurish
reels.top_tip: Intercut actual McGregor vs Holloway fight clips or training footage behind the commentary — adds visual proof that boosts saves (reference value) and justifies the shares

reels.caption:

good: Strong text hook with fighter names and UFC branding immediately visible in frame 1
Excellent average watch time (9.6s) shows pacing keeps viewers engaged through entire clip
High share count (111) demonstrates KO content's viral appeal and shareability
Clean visual composition in early frames with strong fighter silhouettes against cage
bad: Extremely low save rate (3 saves) despite decent engagement—content lacks bookmark-worthy moment
Missing close-up of actual KO impact or fighter's reaction—peaks emotionally in early frame then loses intensity
Mid-video frame cluttered with multiple people around octagon—loses focus on the fight action
No emotional text overlay or retention cue in later frames to drive saves or repeat views
reels.top_tip: Add a slow-motion replay of the actual knockout impact (the moment of connection/finish) as the climax—this creates the dramatic peak that drives saves and rewatches, transforming a 'watch once' reel into 'save and share' content

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 E se o UFC soubesse que Conor McGregor já estava lesionado antes da luta? Cancelar significaria colocar em risco o ma
good: Hook uses curiosity text to stop scroll ('PENSA ISSO QUEMUEL'), compelling commentary angle

Bilingual caption (Portuguese/English) maximizes reach across language audiences

Extremely low save rate (2) despite strong engagement means content feels consumable-once, not valuable-to-return-to
No visible CTA or branding in frames — viewers share but don't know where to follow for more commentary
reels.top_tip: Show Conor McGregor or UFC branding/imagery in the hook frame instead of just a reaction face. The injury narrative is proven gold for shares — but lead with the SUBJECT, not the commentary, to stop more scrollers and increase watch-through rate.

No fight action: only comparison shots and reaction frames, no highlight clips to retain attention

Static framing with no fight footage shown—gesturing at poster lacks visual dynamism of the medium
